name: Chavacano (Ternateño / Bahra)

ancestors.notes: Spanish is the lexifier; written with the Spanish alphabet.
Tagalog substrate; the earliest layer also carries a Portuguese-Malay (Ternate, Moluccas) Mardijker component.

notes: Ternateño Chabacano (locally 'Bahra'), the Spanish-lexifier creole of Ternate, Cavite, Luzon, Philippines — the oldest and most conservative Manila-Bay Chabacano, brought by Mardijker settlers from Ternate (Moluccas) in 1663. STUB: no variety-specific grapheme map is claimed yet. It uses the Spanish-alphabet orthography and shares the core Chabacano phonology of cbk-zam (seseo, yeísmo, ⟨j g⟩ before front vowels = [h], ⟨v⟩ = [b]); parent set to cbk-zam pending a dedicated description. Ternateño-specific traits (a Portuguese-Malay lexical layer, distinct pronoun and TMA systems) are documented in Sippola's APiCS survey but not yet encoded here.

sources.author: Sippola, Eeva
sources.title: Ternate Chabacano structure dataset (APiCS Survey chapter 47)
sources.publisher: Atlas of Pidgin and Creole Language Structures Online (APiCS), eds. Michaelis, Maurer, Haspelmath & Huber
sources.pages: Ternate Chabacano phonology and morphosyntax; Portuguese-Malay Mardijker substrate; oldest Manila-Bay variety.
sources.notes: Primary descriptive source for the Ternate variety; grapheme map to be derived from it.
